data.title: Catalyst Watch: Spotlight on the Fed, Costco earnings, and Nasdaq 100 additions
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - December 8

  • Volatility watch - Bloom Energy (BE) and The Trade Desk (TTD) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Magnitude International (MAGH), Smart Sand (SND), and Haemonetics (HAE), The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Columbus Circle Capital Corp. I (BRR), Twin Hospitality Group (TWNP), and Nutanix (NTNX). Traders have their eyes on highly shorted iRobot (IRBT) and Serve Robotics (SERV) amid the flurry of robotics announcements from the White House.
  • IPO watch - Companies expected to price their IPOs and begin trading next week include Cardinal Infrastructure Group (CDNL), Lumexa Imaging Holdings (LMRI), and Wealthfront Corp. (WLTH). The IPO lock-up period expires on certain blocks of shares of Voyager Technologies (VOYG) and AIRO Group (AIRO).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Toll Brothers (TOL).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Analog Devices (ADI), Albemarle (ALB), UnitedHealth Group (UNH), and Garmin (GRMN).
  • All week - Notable investor conferences during the week include the Barclays Global TMT Conference, Raymond TMT and Consumer Conference, KeyBanc Capital Markets Consumer Conference, ROTH Deer Valley Conference, and Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services Conference. The healthcare sector will have its eyes on the American Society of Hematology Meeting, which will include presentations by Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and Merck (MRK).
  • All day - John Deere (DE) will hold its Analyst Day event. Jefferies expects the company's update on capital discipline and growth optionality to be the key part of the presentation by management.
  • 8:15 a.m. Golden Globe nominations will be announced amid a media landscape being rocked by the announcement that Netflix (NFLX) will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) for $72 billion.
  • 11:00 a.m. The Android Show | XR Edition will be a 30-minute Android XR-focused livestream highlighting all things XR across glasses, headsets, and related form factors, with a heavy emphasis on Gemini-powered experiences. The event is positioned as Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) main showcase for new Android XR platform capabilities following the commercial launch of Samsung's (OTCPK:SSNLF) Galaxy XR headset in the fall.

 

Tuesday - December 9

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Autozone (AZO), Casey's General Stores (CASY), SailPoint (SAIL), and Campbell's Company (CPB). Options trading implies a double-digit move for GameStop (GME) and AeroVironment (AVAV) after they report.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Home Depot's (HD) Investor and Analyst Conference, Graham Holdings' (GHC) Investor Day, The Andersons' (ANDE) Investor Day, CVS Health's (CVS) Investor Day, Elanco's (ELAN) Investor Day, Ligand Pharmaceuticals' (LGND) Investor Day, and an investor update from GE Vernova (GEV).
  • 9:00 a.m. Exxon Mobil (XOM) will hold a webcast for senior leadership to provide an overview of the updated corporate plan.

 

Wednesday - December 10

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Oracle (ORCL), Adobe (ADBE), Chewy (CHWY), and Vail Resorts (MTN). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Planet Labs (PL) and Oxford Industries (OXM) after they report.
  • All day - Shareholders with Electronic Arts (EA) will vote on the $55 billion acquisition offer from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund. The deal is considered the largest leveraged buyout in history.
  • All day - United Natural Foods (UNFI) will hold an Investor Day event.
  • 8:30 a.m. Coinbase Global (COIN) CFO Alesia Haas will participate in a fireside chat at the 53rd Annual Nasdaq London Investor Conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will announce its interest rate decision.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will hold a press conference. Economists will be listening carefully as they assess the Fed's expected interest rate trajectory for 2026.

 

Thursday - December 11

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO), Costco Wholesale (COST), Ciena (CIEN), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU). Options trading implies a double-digit move for RH (RH) and Mitek Systems (MITK) after they report.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Watsco's (WSO) Investor Day, Weyerhaeuser's (WY) Investor Day, Schneider Electric's (OTCPK:SBGSF) Capital Markets Day, Copa Holdings' (COPA) Investor Day, and Suzano's (SUZ) Investor Day.
  • All day - OPEC will publish its monthly oil report.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date on GSK's (GSK) supplemental Gepotidacin treatment.
  • 11:05 a.m. Microsoft (MSFT) will present at the Barclays Global Technology Conference.
  • 5:00 p.m. Costco (COST) will hold its earnings conference call. Investors will be watching for any indications of a special dividend or stock split in the near future. Of interest, Central Garden & Pet (CENT) generates more than 10% of its revenue from Costco and has traded in tandem with it after earnings 88% of the time over the last two years.

 

Friday - December 12

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Johnson Outdoors (JOUT).
  • All day - The FDA action date for BioCryst Pharmaceuticals' (BCRX) Orladeyo (berotralstat) could lead to a share price movement.
  • Postmarket - Nasdaq 100 index changes are expected to be announced based on the schedule used in the past.

 

Senior Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: A tech valuation reset is ‘the big story’ for 2026 – BofA’s Subramanian
data.description:
Savita Subramanian, head of equity and quant strategy at BofA Securities, is warning of a potential valuation reset for mega-cap technology companies (VGT), (IYW), (XLK), (MAGS) despite projecting strong earnings growth.

In an interview with CNBC, Subramanian expressed concern that investors are “buying the dream” with tech stocks, paying peak or near-peak multiples for companies building out power infrastructure and data centers for artificial intelligence without clear evidence of near-term monetization.

“We’re paying a high multiple for growth stocks, but we don’t exactly know how this all plays out over the next few years,” she said, noting that real monetization of AI investments may not materialize until 2026 due to power constraints and implementation challenges.

While Subramanian forecasts robust 14% earnings growth for next year, reaching $310 per share, she believes markets won’t necessarily follow suit.

“The valuation reset is the big story for next year,” she said, adding that “historically, if you look at years of really strong earnings growth, you haven’t necessarily seen the strongest market gains.”

Contrary to market consensus, Subramanian recommends increasing exposure to consumer staples (XLP) and discount retailers (WMT), (DG), (TJX), (ROST)…

She believes the lower-income consumer segment could see improvement in the months ahead due to several factors, including potentially higher tax refunds and government efforts to address inflation ahead of the midterm elections.

data.title: U.S. stocks in the green as NFLX-WBD acquisition fuels rally, investors assess inflation gauge
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Friday, as traders assessed fresh inflation data in hopes of gauging the Federal Reserve's monetary policy, while news of Netflix ((NFLX)) acquiring Warner Bros. Discovery ((WBD)) dominated news.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.3%. 

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) -2.4% announced it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) +5.1%, including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO, for a total enterprise value of ~$82.7 billion.

“This creates basically a two-horse show. Disney (DIS), with physical assets and parks, similar to Comcast (CMCSA); and Netflix (NFLX) with tons of streaming and movie assets. Paramount (PSKY) [is] stuck now treading water. [David Ellison, CEO of Paramount Skydance] is the big loser here. Cable assets will all be shed from all. Disney needs to do the same next,” said Ross Gerber, co-founder, CEO and CIO of Gerber Kawasaki.

In economic news, core PCE inflation stalled at +0.2% MoM in September as expected. Meanwhile, December consumer sentiment was higher at 53.3 vs. 52.0 consensus and 51.0 in November, according to the initial UofM Survey.

“Income [is] up 0.4%, spending 0.3%. However, inflation adjusted spending was flat which implied a soft spending path heading into the holiday spending season,” said Joseph Brusuelas, RSM U.S. principal and chief economist.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 5 basis points to 3.58%.

data.title: Tech Voices: NYT vs. Perplexity, SoftBank-DigitalBridge, SpaceX
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • The New York Times (NYT) is suing Perplexity for allegedly copying and distributing its copyrighted content.

“While we believe in the ethical and responsible use and development of AI, we firmly object to Perplexity’s unlicensed use of our content to develop and promote their products,” NYT spokesperson Graham James said in a statement, according to CNBC. “We will continue to work to hold companies accountable that refuse to recognize the value of our work.”

CNBC noted that the NYT is also suing OpenAI (OPENAI) and Microsoft (MSFT) for using its content to train AI models.

  • SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) is reportedly in discussions to buy private equity group DigitalBridge (DBRG), which has been investing in data centers, and take it private.

According to Bloomberg, a deal could be announced in the coming weeks. DigitalBridge's portfolio includes AIMS, AtlasEdge, DataBank, Switch, Vantage Data Centers, and Yondr Group.

  • SpaceX (SPACE) is planning a secondary share sale that values the company at $800 billion, which would make it the most valuable private company in the U.S., according to The Wall Street Journal.

The company had been valued at $400 billion during a secondary share sale in July, the WSJ added.

SpaceX is controlled by Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk.

data.title: Albemarle upgraded to Buy at UBS as lithium seen entering new upcycle
data.description:
Albemarle (ALB) +6.5% in Friday's trading as UBS upgraded shares to Buy from Neutral with a $185 price target, raised from $107, seeing a combination of higher energy storage demand and years of slower western capacity additions now pushing lithium markets into deficit in 2026.

UBS analyst Joshua Spector said he thinks a tighter market should lift lithium prices $2-$3/kg over the next 6-9 months, and "a market in deficit would push prices back to reinvestment levels and risk a price fly up scenario - we now think that is possible in 2027."

Spector expects lithium prices to move up through 2026, which should be a positive driver for Albemarle (ALB) shares, and he raised his estimates for 2026, '27 and '28 EBITDA on higher pricing to $1.8 billion, $3.5 billion and $3.3 billion, respectively, and well above Wall Street consensus.

The analyst also noted a potential upside scenario for Albemarle (ALB) if the U.S. views lithium as strategic and considers a price floor to support new projects, which would be positive for the company's Kings Mountain resource and support a two-tier lithium price model that could benefit ~35% of total production.

data.title: Strong Q3 and upbeat holiday outlook highlight Ulta's resilience in competitve market -- analysts
data.description:
Ulta (ULTA) put in another strong quarter and raised its outlook for a second time, underscoring the resilience of the beauty retailer in a highly competitive market.

Led by double-digit sales growth in most sub-categories (fragrances, skincare, haircare, cosmetics), revenue grew 13% thanks to much better-than-expected comparable store sales and the acquisition of Space NK that expands the company’s presence in the UK and Ireland.

The results further showcased the success of Ulta Beauty’s (ULTA) loyalty program, whose 45 million members generate 95% of the company’s sales.

“For a retailer, this is a very important fact because it means that it is no longer necessary to chase customers every quarter with new offers and promotions,” Seek Alpha analyst Agar Capital  says.

Along with strong Q3 results, the stock price was propped up by the outlook for the pivotal holiday season.

“We think there is room for upside to the guided Q4 comp of 2.5% to 3.5% given the positive start to the holiday season with sold Black Friday/Cyber Monday trends,” says Morgan Stanley’s Simeon Gutman.

Management’s guidance appears conservative, Seeking Alpha analyst Sandpiper Investment Research writes, as “margin expansion and share repurchases provide further upside potential.”

“In my view, that gap [between FY25 comp guidance of 4.4%-4.7% and Q4 of 2.5%-3.5%] seems like some sandbagging where the fragrance shelf expansions, exclusive gift sets, and digital momentum all feel stronger than a year ago, so I would not be shocked to see the quarter land closer to 4%[comp sales] instead.”

Looking further ahead to 2026, CEO Kecia Steelman said on the company's earnings call that next year will not be another investment cycle and operating margins will remain steady to 2025 levels.

But the company’s long-term prospects remain favorable, Oppenheimer’s Rupesh Parikh says, thanks to the company’s differentiated offering and unique value proposition, position in the beauty category, potential for above-average growth, and ongoing market share potential.

“Ulta remains a top pick for us, and we still see strong outperformance case following major gains year to date,” Parikh says, reiterating an Outperform rating with a new $615 price target.

Ulta (ULTA) shares are up 32.5% this year, outperforming a 16.6% gain for the S&P 500.

data.title: Anheuser-Busch acquires a rising star in the ready-to-drink alcoholic beverage category
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) announced plans to acquire a majority stake in BeatBox, which it called a rising star in the ready-to-drink beverage category that is known for its bold, fruit-forward Party Punch flavors. 

The beer giant is acquiring 85% of BeatBox for a purchase price of up to approximately $490 million with a path to 100% ownership after five years based on a predetermined pricing formula. This transaction is subject to regulatory approval and customary closing conditions, and it is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026.  BeatBox originated in Austin, Texas, and gained popularity after its founders' appearance on Shark Tank in 2014. The ready-to-drink cocktail brand is now known for its lively "party-in-a-box" approach, offering bold, fruit-forward flavors like Party Punch, Blue Razzberry, and Peach Punch, as well as Hard Tea and Lemonade options. The beverages are primarily wine-based but also come in malt-based variants, with alcohol by volume levels typically at 11.1% or 8.0%. BeatBox's drinks are vegan, use certified food colorings, and are designed to be smooth and non-carbonated.

Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) said the deal adds a world-class brand to its fast-growing Beyond Beer portfolio, which already includes Cutwater Spirits, NÜTRL Vodka Seltzer, and Phorm Energy. As a top 10 RTD brand in the industry, BeatBox was said to complement A-B's Beyond Beer portfolio and bring flavors and styles that are incremental to the company’s current RTD offerings. 

"We have a proven playbook for building winning brands, and I look forward to partnering with BeatBox and embarking on their next chapter of dynamic growth together," highlighted Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) CEO Brendan Whitworth.

Last month, TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow said a deal for BeatBox would serve as further confirmation that the big beer companies will need to acquire more emerging beyond-beer brands to offset rising structural headwinds in the beer industry.

data.title: Victoria's Secret raises 2025 sales outlook to $6.45B-$6.48B amid double-digit international growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Victoria's Secret & Co. (VSCO) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Hillary Super, CEO, reported, "we delivered standout third quarter results with outperformance on the top and bottom lines that far exceeded the high end of our guidance." She highlighted the success of the Path to Potential strategy, which drove gains in the intimates business, noting, "our intimates business returned to growth, up mid-single digits, resulting in us gaining over 1% share in the U.S. intimates market." Super emphasized customer acquisition as a new focus, with the total customer file growing for the first time this year, led by new customers. The Victoria's Secret Fashion Show was highlighted as a major driver of brand engagement, leading to "tangible business impact, particularly in our intimates business." International business saw over 30% sales growth, with China as a standout market. The CEO stated, "we are raising our outlook for the year," citing strong holiday performance and increased customer turnout.
  • Scott Sekella, Chief Financial and Operating Officer, stated, "Net sales for the quarter were $1.472 billion, an increase of $125 million or 9% over last year with comparable sales growth of 8%, exceeding the high end of our guidance." Sekella added, "adjusted operating income for the third quarter was breakeven, which was better than our guidance of an adjusted operating loss of $35 million to $55 million."

Outlook

  • Sekella announced, "we are raising our full year outlook for net sales and are now forecasting net sales in the range of $6.45 billion to $6.48 billion compared to our prior guidance calling for net sales in the range of $6.33 billion to $6.41 billion." The company is also increasing its full year adjusted operating income guidance to $350 million to $375 million. Fourth quarter net sales are forecasted to be between $2.17 billion and $2.2 billion. Adjusted net income per diluted share for fiscal year 2025 is now expected to be in the range of $2.40 to $2.65, up from previous guidance of $1.80 to $2.20. Inventory is expected to be up mid-teens percent in Q4 to support business trends and tariff impacts.

Financial Results

  • The company reported net sales of $1.47 billion for the third quarter, up 9% from the previous year. Comparable sales grew 8%. Adjusted gross margin expanded by 170 basis points to 36.5%. International sales grew 34% to $265 million, with China driving much of the growth. Adjusted SG&A rate was 36.5%, improving by 30 basis points versus the prior year. Adjusted net loss per share was ($0.27), significantly better than guidance of ($0.55) to ($0.75) and last year's ($0.50) per share. Cash balance at quarter-end was $249 million.

Q&A

  • Mauricio Serna Vega, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: Asked about maintaining momentum post Fashion Show and market share opportunities. Super responded that the company is seeing continued strong traffic and momentum, especially in bras and glamor, with a "full pipeline of innovation in the bra world in particular as well as in the beauty world" for 2026.
  • Brooke Roach, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about new customer acquisition demographics. Super shared, "we are seeing it distorted to 18- to 24-year-olds. So that was our goal. We are seeing that come to life." Roach also questioned EBIT margin targets. Sekella replied, "we definitely still see a low double-digit operating margin as achievable. I think the question is when over the next couple of years."
  • Adrienne Yih, Barclays: Asked about pipeline for bra launches and PINK trends. Super detailed a focus on frequent launches and noted, "this is just the beginning of bra growth for us as a total company."
  • Matthew Boss, JPMorgan: Requested insights on market share and momentum. Super stated, "we can continue to perform and grow market share despite the market... we are the #1 market shareholder."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a positive to slightly positive tone, congratulating management on results and focusing on growth momentum, customer acquisition, and margin expansion. Questions pressed for sustainability of gains and details on strategy.
  • Management’s tone was confident in prepared remarks, stating, "we feel confident in our ability to execute but are being thoughtful about the consumer, particularly post holiday." During Q&A, confidence remained with frequent references to ongoing innovation and strategic execution, while acknowledging some caution regarding the broader consumer environment post-holiday.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone has shifted from cautious optimism to more assertive confidence, and analysts’ tone moved from curiosity about turnaround to recognition of improved performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year net sales and adjusted net income per share has been raised from the prior quarter. International growth accelerated, particularly in China. Intimates returned to growth, and PINK delivered double-digit sales increases, outperforming the previous quarter’s low single-digit gains. Gross margin and SG&A rates showed improvement. Margin expansion and market share gains were more pronounced in Q3. Management’s tone shifted to greater confidence, and analyst focus moved from turnaround prospects to sustainability and scale of recent gains.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing tariff headwinds, with mitigation strategies including vendor cost optimization, diversified sourcing, and pricing adjustments. Sekella noted, "our guidance for the full year 2025 now assumes net tariff impact of approximately $90 million, with approximately $65 million impacting the fourth quarter." Caution was expressed regarding the consumer environment post-holiday. Inventory increases were highlighted as necessary to support growth and manage tariffs.

Final Takeaway

Victoria’s Secret & Co. leadership emphasized standout third quarter results, driven by market share gains in intimates, robust international growth, and strong execution of the Path to Potential strategy. The success of the Fashion Show and customer engagement initiatives fueled new and reactivated customer acquisition, especially in key younger demographics. With raised guidance for net sales and earnings, the company projects continued momentum into the holiday season despite tariff pressures, supported by product innovation and operational leverage.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SpaceX’s latest valuation makes it the world's most valuable private company
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) is preparing a secondary share sale that would value the company at $800 billion, sources told The Wall Street Journal. Chief Financial Officer Bret Johnsen reportedly told investors about the sale earlier this week. The $800 billion valuation is double the reported $400 million valuation from a sale of employee stock earlier this year.

The valuation of SpaceX (SPACE) has seen dramatic growth since its founding in 2002. Initially, the space technology company was valued at just $27 million. By 2015, after major investments from Google (GOOG) and Fidelity, the valuation had climbed to around $12 billion. The launch and rapid expansion of the Starlink (STRLK) satellite constellation, along with successful rocket launches and contracts with NASA, fueled further growth. In 2019, the valuation of the Elon Musk-led company reached about $33.3 billion. A significant leap occurred in August 2020, when a major funding round pushed the valuation to $46 billion. 

The momentum continued into 2021, with a valuation of $74 billion in February and surpassing $100 billion by October after secondary share sales. In 2022, the company's valuation was reported at $127 billion. The pace of growth accelerated even further, with SpaceX (SPACE) valued at $150 billion in July 2023, $180 billion in December 2023, and $210 billion by June 2024. 

A new $800 billion valuation on SpaceX (SPACE) would make it the most valuable private company in the world, topping the private market valuations for OpenAI (OPENAI), Tether, ByteDance (BDNCE), and Anthropic (ANTHRO).

data.title: DigitalBridge may be worth $25-$35/share in a sale - analyst
data.description:
DigitialBridge (DBRG) could fetch $25-$35 in a potential sale after a report that Softbank (OTCPK:SFTBY) was in talks to purchase the company.

Bloomberg earlier Friday reported that Softbank (SFTBTY)  is in talks to acquire DigitalBridge (DBRG).

DigitalBridge (DBRG) could see $25 a share based on conservative estimates, with potential upside to $30 to $35, JPMorgan analyst Richard Choe wrote in a note on Friday. 

"We see an attrractive takeout price of $28 per share on our 2026 estimates, but a high-end valuation range of $30-$35 per share if we look to our 2027 estimates on a premium multiple," Choe, who has an "overweight" rating on the shares, wrote in the note.

DigitalBridge shares soared 44% on news of the potential takeover.
